definition: Section 2244(b)(1)'s prohibition on raising a claim in a successive habeas petition that was already presented in a prior petition. It is an absolute bar — unlike (b)(2), which allows new claims meeting certain criteria — and prevents prisoners from relitigating claims that have already been decided.
examples: Six circuits apply the do-over bar to federal prisoners, preventing them from raising previously presented claims in successive Section 2255 motions.
